learning_outcomes.description: Explain relevant interactions of physics, chemistry and biology relevant to water and wastewater treatment processes.
Examine given water or wastewater treatment processes to discuss key objectives of operation and control and develop appropriate control strategies.
Solve process models computationally. Analyze and debug model code, obtain and analyze outputs to assess model performance and achieve model study objectives.
Analyze and adapt the model in response to field data. Conduct parameter estimation and model validation using field data.
Use models to evaluate water and wastewater treatment options and select and defend water and wastewater solutions.
Apply models to analyse process dynamic behaviour and use this to devise control and optimisation solutions.

assessments.task_description:
The duration of the written exam will be Reading: 10 minutes; Duration: 60 minutes.

Closed book. Casio FX82 series only or UQ approved and labelled calculator required.

Invigilated In-class exam. External students are invigilated via Zoom.

This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT). Students are advised that the use of AI or MT technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

assessments.task_description:
Identity Verified Assessment. Closed book. Students are required to achieve a minimum final exam score of 40% to achieve a passing grade.

This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT). Students are advised that the use of AI or MT technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

Students enrolled in internal mode will have an invigilated (supervised) examination on campus. To ensure equity of treatment across both internal and external modes, and to maintain academic integrity of the external mode examination, students who are enrolled in external mode will have an invigilated (supervised) online examination. Supervision will be via Zoom for external students, please see the external delivery course profile for further details on online invigilated exams materials and requirements.
